steps.title: Identify the embedded question
steps.content: The sentence contains a question clause (“what [blank]”) embedded within a main statement, not a direct question. This is an indirect question.

steps.title: Apply the indirect question rule
steps.content: Indirect questions require statement word order (subject-verb), not question word order (auxiliary-subject-verb). “he viewed” correctly uses statement word order in “what he viewed as unnecessary development.” Choices C and D violate this rule with “did he”; choice B misuses a question mark mid-sentence. That matches choice A — A is correct.
